---
title: Other Data
---

Beyond [parameters](/docs/hdf/params), [metadata](/docs/hdf/metadata),
[series](/docs/hdf/series), and [meshes](/docs/hdf/meshes), you can store any arbitrary
data directly in the HDF5 hierarchy using the generic [Group and Dataset](/docs/hdf/objects)
API exposed through `sim.root`.

## Storing custom groups and datasets

`sim.root` is a [`Group`](/apidocs/core/hdf5/ref#Group) pointing at `/` inside `data.h5`.
Navigate and create sub-groups exactly as you would with `h5py`, but through the
`bamboost` wrappers that handle lazy file access and MPI safely.

```python
import numpy as np

with sim.edit() as writer:
    # Create a group (idempotent, like h5py.require_group)
    stats = writer.root.require_group("statistics")

    # Store an attribute on the group
    stats.attrs["final_residual"] = 3.2e-6

    # Store a numpy array as a dataset
    stats["residual_history"] = np.array([1e-1, 5e-2, 1e-2, 5e-3, 1e-3])

    # Nest groups as deeply as you need
    checkpoints = stats.require_group("checkpoints")
    checkpoints["iterations"] = np.array([10, 20, 30], dtype=np.int64)
```

## Reading custom data back

```python
sim = coll["my-simulation"]

stats = sim.root["statistics"]

# Read an attribute
residual = stats.attrs["final_residual"]

# Read a dataset (lazy reference—slice to load into memory)
history = stats["residual_history"][:]

# Read a nested dataset
iters = sim.root["statistics/checkpoints/iterations"][:]
```

## Git snapshot

`bamboost` includes a built-in helper to record the current state of a git repository
alongside the simulation data. This is useful for full reproducibility.

```python
with sim.edit() as writer:
    writer.git.add("my_solver", repo_path="/path/to/solver")
```

This stores the remote origin URL, the current commit hash, the active branch, and
a `git diff HEAD` patch in the `.git` group inside `data.h5`.

```python
# Read back git information
git_item = sim.git["my_solver"]
print(git_item.commit)   # e.g. "a3b1f29..."
print(git_item.branch)   # e.g. "main"
print(git_item.origin)   # e.g. "https://github.com/..."
print(git_item.patch)    # the diff as a string
```
